topics: Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter (solid, liquid, gas); change of state
Elements, compounds and mixtures; Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures; Colloids and suspensions
Physical changes; Chemical changes
Law of Chemical Combination; Chemical formulae; Atomic and molecular masses
Sub-atomic particles; Models of atom; Valency; Atomic Number and Mass Number; Isotopes and Isobars
Prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; Multicellular organisms
Cell membrane; Cell wall; Nucleus; Chromosomes
Animal tissues; Plant tissues (Meristematic and Permanent); Organs
Organization of Organ Systems
Distance and displacement; Velocity; Uniform and non-uniform motion; Acceleration
Newton's Laws of Motion; Inertia; Momentum
Universal Law of Gravitation; Gravity; Acceleration due to Gravity
Thrust; Pressure; Archimedes' Principle
Work done by a Force; Kinetic and Potential energy
Nature of sound; Propagation in media; Speed of sound
Breeding for quality improvement; Management
Use of fertilizers; Use of manures
Protection from pests; Protection from diseases
Principles of organic farming

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Physical and Chemical Changes (derived from topics)

topics: Types of chemical reactions (combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation); endothermic/exothermic; oxidation/reduction
Definitions in terms of H+ and OH- ions; indicators; neutralization; pH scale; importance of pH
Properties of metals/non-metals; Reactivity series; Ionic compounds; Metallurgical processes
Covalent bonds; Versatile nature of carbon; Hydrocarbons (saturated/unsaturated); Homologous series
Nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals

chapters.source_heading: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
topics: Tropic movements; plant hormones; Nervous system (Voluntary, involuntary, reflex); Chemical co-ordination
Reproduction in animals/plants (asexual/sexual); reproductive health; family planning methods
Heredity; Mendel's contribution; Laws for inheritance of traits; Sex determination
Reflection of light by curved surfaces; Images formed by spherical mirrors
Laws of refraction; refractive index; Refraction through spherical lens
Functioning of human eye; defects of vision and corrections
Electric current; potential difference; Ohm's law; Resistance and Resistivity
Eco-system; Environmental problems (Ozone depletion); waste production

suspicious_chapter_names: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants

practicals.title: Preparation of solutions (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 1. Preparation of: a) true solution of common salt, sugar and alum b) suspension of soil, chalk powder and fine sand c) colloidal solution of starch in water and egg albumin/milk

practicals.title: Preparation of mixture vs compound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 2. Preparation of a) A mixture b) A compound using iron filings and sulphur powder

practicals.title: Physical or chemical changes (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 3. Perform the following reactions: Iron with copper sulphate; Burning of magnesium ribbon; Zinc with dilute sulphuric acid; Heating of copper sulphate crystals; Sodium sulphate with barium chloride

practicals.title: Stained temporary mounts (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 4. Preparation of stained temporary mounts of onion peel, human cheek cells & to record observations and draw their labeled diagrams

practicals.title: Identification of tissues (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 5. Identification of Parenchyma, Collenchyma and Sclerenchyma tissues in plants; striped, smooth and cardiac muscle fibers and nerve cells

practicals.title: Melting point of ice and boiling point of water (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 6. Determination of the melting point of ice and the boiling point of water.

practicals.title: Laws of reflection of sound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 7. Verification of the laws of reflection of sound.

practicals.title: Density of solid (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 8. Determination of the density of solid (denser than water) by using a spring balance and a measuring cylinder.

practicals.title: Archimedes' Principle verification (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 9. Establishing the relation between the loss in weight of a solid when fully immersed in Tap water / Strongly salty water with the weight of water displaced

practicals.title: Speed of pulse (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of the speed of a pulse propagated through a stretched string/slinky.

practicals.title: Conservation of mass (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 11. Verification of the law of conservation of mass in a chemical reaction.

practicals.title: Finding pH (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 1A. Finding the pH of Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, NaOH solution, Ethanoic Acid, Lemon juice, Water, Hydrogen Carbonate solution

practicals.title: Properties of acids and bases (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 1B. Studying the properties of acids and bases on reaction with Litmus solution, Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate

practicals.title: Types of reactions (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 2. Performing and observing: Action of water on quicklime; Heat on ferrous sulphate crystals; Iron nails in copper sulphate solution; Sodium sulphate with barium chloride

practicals.title: Reactivity series (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 3. Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on salt solutions; Arranging in decreasing order of reactivity

practicals.title: Ohm's Law verification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 4. Studying the dependence of potential difference across a resistor on current; plotting V-I graph and determining resistance.

practicals.title: Equivalent resistance (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 5. Determination of the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata observation (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 6. Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Respiration experiment (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 7. Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Properties of acetic acid (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 8. Study properties: Odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate

practicals.title: Soap cleaning capacity (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 9. Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal length determination (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of the focal length of Concave mirror and Convex lens by obtaining image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Refraction through glass slab (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 11. Tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence.

practicals.title: Binary fission and Budding (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 12. Studying binary fission in Amoeba, budding in yeast and Hydra with prepared slides.

practicals.title: Refraction through prism (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 13. Tracing the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo parts identification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 14. Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed.

activities.title: Periodic Assessment (Class IX/X)
activities.source_text: Three periodic tests conducted by the school. Average of best two tests to be taken that will have a weightage of 05 marks towards the final result.

activities.title: Diverse Assessment Methods (Class IX/X)
activities.source_text: Short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations and enquiry based scientific investigations etc.

activities.title: Portfolio Maintenance
activities.source_text: Includes classwork and other sample of student's work. Weightage 05 marks towards final result.

projects.title: Project Assessment
projects.source_text: Projects are listed under diverse methods of assessment for Internal Assessment (05 marks weightage). Schools can integrate these with existing chapters.

excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Information in NCERT text book boxes (not assessed in year-end examination)
Separating the components of a mixture (Physical and chemical changes topic, Class IX)
Commercial unit of Energy (Work, Energy and Power topic, Class IX)
Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale (Class X)
teacher_notes: The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.
Practicals should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes.
All practical work listed in the syllabus must be completed and maintained as a record by students.
Formative topics (Health/Diseases, Conservation of Momentum) are included to reinforce understanding without adding summative stress; schools can integrate these with existing chapters.
